summary: Russia launched a large-scale overnight drone attack on Ukraine with nearly 200 drones, striking Odesa, Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, and Kherson regions, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage. Ukrainian drones struck a marine terminal in St. Petersburg, with video showing the burning facility and Russian forces attempting to shoot down the UAV. On the eastern front, Russian forces are intensifying efforts in the Krasnaya Liman, Slavyansk, and Konstantinovka sectors, with urban battles ongoing and advances reported near Yurkovka and Ray-Aleksandrovka.
key_points: Russia attacked Ukraine overnight with nearly 200 drones targeting Odesa, Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, and Kherson regions, killing one woman and wounding at least ten in Kherson alone. (Source: @UkraineNow/81639)
Ukrainian drones struck a marine terminal in St. Petersburg, with footage showing the burning facility while Russian National Guard troops attempted to shoot down the UAV. (Source: @eRadarrua/95151)

Russian forces are expanding control in the Kostyantynovka sector, with Ukrainian units experiencing infantry shortages as reinforcements are diverted to forming new brigades. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30628)

In the Krasnaya Liman area, urban battles are underway with massive UAV use; Russian forces are advancing along the M-03 highway toward Yurkovka, 12 km from Slavyansk outskirts. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30635)
A Ukrainian drone struck a Moscow-Simferopol passenger bus, killing 7 people. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30637)

Multiple UAV groups were tracked across northern and central Ukraine heading toward Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Synelnykove, and other locations, with air raid alerts active across several oblasts. (Source: @kpszsu/63702)
Zelensky stated that all Russian missile types require foreign components, citing 145 components per Kalibr missile and over 17,000 components for 650 strike drones. (Source: @UkraineNow/81633)
Former NATO Secretary General Stoltenberg said Europe is mentally unprepared for a major war and that NATO's current situation is more complex than in 2024. (Source: @UkraineNow/81635)

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a massive overnight assault on Ukraine with nearly 200 drones, striking civilian infrastructure across Odesa, Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, and Kherson regions, resulting in multiple civilian casualties including one fatality in Kherson. Simultaneously, Ukrainian long-range drones struck a marine terminal in St. Petersburg, demonstrating continued deep-strike capability against Russian territory. On the eastern front, Russian forces are intensifying offensive operations in the Krasnaya Liman, Slavyansk, and Konstantinovka sectors, with urban battles and advances reported along key axes.

points: Ukraine was struck by nearly 200 Russian drones overnight. In Odesa region, a truck and service building were damaged, with two men injured, one hospitalized. In Zaporizhzhia region, an oil depot was hit causing a fire, with no casualties reported. (Source: @UkraineNow/81639)

In Dnipropetrovsk region, Russian forces attacked over 15 times with drones and artillery targeting Nikopolsky, Kamyansky, and Samarsky districts. A residential house, infrastructure facilities, an agricultural enterprise, and a shop were damaged, with no casualties. (Source: @UkraineNow/81639)

In Kherson, Russian forces struck residential quarters with artillery and drones across Dnipro, Korabelny, and Central districts. One woman was killed and at least ten people wounded, including a 17-year-old boy. (Source: @UkraineNow/81639)

Ukrainian drones struck a marine terminal in St. Petersburg, with video footage showing the facility burning. Russian National Guard troops were seen attempting to shoot down the UAV with automatic weapons while others filmed the scene. (Source: @eRadarrua/95151)
A Ukrainian drone struck a Moscow-Simferopol passenger bus, resulting in the deaths of 7 people. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30637)
Ukrainian Air Force spokesman Ignat stated that the shortage of air defense missiles has persisted throughout the entire war. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30612)

President Zelensky stated that all Russian missile types cannot be produced without foreign components, citing 145 components per Kalibr, 1,122 per 33 Iskander missiles, and over 17,000 components for 650 strike drones. (Source: @UkraineNow/81633)

A marine terminal in St. Petersburg was struck by a Ukrainian drone and caught fire. Snipers were deployed on rooftops across the city, and internet was reportedly shut down in anticipation of the attack. (Source: @eRadarrua/95156)
A Moscow-Simferopol passenger bus was struck by a Ukrainian drone, killing 7 people. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30637)

Russia launched nearly 200 drones in an overnight assault on Ukraine targeting Odesa, Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, and Kherson regions. Three additional Shahed-type drones were reported heading toward Kyiv from the north. (Source: @UkraineNow/81639)

EU Ambassador to Ukraine Katarina Maternova stated Russia is fighting by squeezing out its last forces, with financial resources, human resources, strength, and motivation running out. (Source: @UkraineNow/81619)

Russia established an alternative 'International Public Tribunal' headed by Russian propagandist Maxim Grigoryev, including American John Dugan and far-right activist Charles Bousman, in an attempt to discredit the future Special Tribunal in The Hague. (Source: @UkraineNow/81632)

In the Krasnaya Liman area, urban battles are taking place with massive UAV use by both sides. Northwest of there, battles are ongoing on the outskirts of Sviatogorsk, with Russian forces intensifying efforts in this direction. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30635)

On the Slavyansk front, Russian forces are advancing along the M-03 highway toward Yurkovka, located 12 km from Slavyansk outskirts. Battles are reported in Ray-Aleksandrovka, an important Ukrainian defensive hub. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30635)

In Konstantinovka, intense urban battles continue. Russian forces are expanding their zone of control in the Kostyantynovka sector, where Ukrainian units face infantry shortages. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30628)

In the Dnipropetrovsk region, the GR 'Vostok' group is advancing in the forested area beyond the Volchya River northwest of Oleksandrihrad, consolidating and expanding a bridgehead north of the river. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30635)
Multiple UAV groups were tracked heading toward Kharkiv from the south and from the east toward Izyum in Kharkiv region. UAVs were also reported near Bohodukhiv heading south. (Source: @kpszsu/63703)

Ukrainian Commander-in-Chief has reduced issuance of reinforcements to active units under the pretext of forming new brigades, with reports of half the personnel in one such brigade going AWOL due to poor command conditions. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30628)
A reactive-type UAV was reported in northern Zhytomyr region heading toward Rivne region. Another UAV was reported in northern Rivne region heading west. (Source: @eRadarrua/95142)
In Sumy region, new UAV groups were detected crossing the border from Kursk Oblast heading toward Terny. (Source: @kpszsu/63727)
Three Shahed-type drones were reported heading toward Kyiv from the north, with at least two tracked heading toward central areas. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67464)

In Kherson, Russian forces struck residential quarters with artillery and drones across Dnipro, Korabelny, and Central districts. One woman was killed and at least ten people wounded, including a 17-year-old boy. (Source: @UkraineNow/81639)

UAVs were reported over Zaporizhzhia region near Ternuvate-Novomykolaivka, heading toward Zaporizhzhia from the east. Another UAV group was tracked from Kherson region toward Berezneguvate in Mykolaiv region. (Source: @kpszsu/63732)
A UAV was detected in the Black Sea waters near Chornomorsk. In Mykolaiv, a UAV was reported over the city with residents advised to take shelter. (Source: @kpszsu/63761)
In the Zaporozhye and Kherson regions, the situation remains unchanged but the logistical situation remains difficult. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30635)

Russia launched nearly 200 drones overnight against Ukraine, with strikes across Odesa, Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, and Kherson regions. Three additional Shahed drones were reported heading toward Kyiv from the north. (Source: @UkraineNow/81639)

Multiple UAV groups were tracked across Ukraine: toward Kharkiv from south and east, toward Synelnykove from south, toward Hirske in Dnipropetrovsk region, toward Zaporizhzhia from east, and from Kherson toward Mykolaiv region. (Source: @kpszsu/63702)
Ukrainian Air Force spokesman Ignat stated the shortage of air defense missiles has persisted throughout the entire war. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30612)
A NATO reconnaissance aircraft was circling in the area of Kaliningrad Oblast, using Lithuanian and Polish airspace and neutral waters of the Baltic Sea. (Source: @tass_agency/378793)
A drone-relay type UAV was identified near Sofiyivka, flying at approximately 150 km/h and circling the area about ten times. (Source: @eRadarrua/95171)

key_developments: Russia launched a massive overnight drone assault on Ukraine with nearly 200 drones, striking civilian infrastructure across Odesa, Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, and Kherson regions, killing one woman in Kherson and wounding at least ten others there, plus additional casualties in Odesa. (Source: @UkraineNow/81639)

Ukrainian drones struck a marine terminal in St. Petersburg, setting it ablaze, with video showing Russian National Guard troops attempting to shoot down the UAV with automatic weapons while the facility burned. (Source: @eRadarrua/95151)

Russian forces are intensifying offensive operations on the eastern front, with urban battles in Krasnaya Liman and Konstantinovka, advances along the M-03 highway toward Yurkovka (12 km from Slavyansk), and fighting in Ray-Aleksandrovka, an important Ukrainian defensive hub. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30635)

Russian forces are expanding control in the Kostyantynovka sector where Ukrainian units face infantry shortages, as the Ukrainian Commander-in-Chief redirects reinforcements to forming new brigades, with reports of mass AWOL and poor conditions in some units. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30628)
A Ukrainian drone struck a Moscow-Simferopol passenger bus, killing 7 people. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30637)

Multiple UAV groups were tracked across Ukraine heading toward Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Synelnykove, Kyiv, and other locations, with three Shahed-type drones reported heading toward Kyiv from the north. (Source: @kpszsu/63703)

President Zelensky stated all Russian missile types require foreign components, citing 145 components per Kalibr, 1,122 per 33 Iskander missiles, and over 17,000 components for 650 strike drones, calling this complicity in killings. (Source: @UkraineNow/81633)
sections.threat_assessment: The operational tempo is escalating on both sides, with Russia conducting one of its larger overnight drone campaigns (nearly 200 drones) while Ukraine demonstrates continued deep-strike capability against St. Petersburg and Russian transport targets. The eastern front is seeing intensified Russian urban warfare in Krasnaya Liman, Konstantinovka, and along the Slavyansk axis, with Ukrainian forces facing infantry shortages exacerbated by command decisions to redirect personnel to new brigade formations. The persistent Ukrainian air defense missile shortage, acknowledged by Air Force spokesman Ignat, remains a critical vulnerability as Russia sustains large-scale drone and missile campaigns. The EU ambassador's assessment that Russia is exhausting its resources suggests a war of attrition dynamic, though Russia's current offensive intensity on the eastern front indicates it retains significant combat capability.
sections.regional_response: Former NATO Secretary General Stoltenberg warned that Europe remains mentally unprepared for a major war despite witnessing conflict on its doorstep, and characterized NATO's current situation as more complex than when he left office in 2024. The US is rotating its military contingent in Lithuania, which was increased to 1,000 soldiers with armored vehicles and heavy weapons after the start of the conflict, though the timing and composition of the replacement contingent remain unknown. NATO reconnaissance aircraft continue monitoring the Kaliningrad Oblast region using Baltic airspace. Russia established a propaganda 'International Public Tribunal' headed by Maxim Grigoryev to discredit future international legal proceedings in The Hague.
sections.intelligence_notes: A new NATO military expert council called ARES (Allied Reform and Expert Support) was created in April 2026, comprising retired military leaders from North America and Europe to advise Ukrainian Armed Forces on reforms and combat operations, while also adopting Ukrainian war experience for NATO's own reform. Ukrainian Air Force spokesman Ignat's admission of a persistent air defense missile shortage throughout the war raises questions about the effectiveness of Western air defense supplies. The GR 'Vostok' group's advance in forested terrain beyond the Volchya River northwest of Oleksandrihrad suggests Russian forces are seeking to establish bridgeheads in less expected terrain. Reports of half the personnel in a newly formed Ukrainian brigade going AWOL due to poor command conditions indicate ongoing organizational challenges within the Ukrainian military structure.
